redeem.title: Convert CFT to USDC
redeem.intro: Choose how much CFT you want to redeem, review the live USDC estimate, and complete the conversion through a one-transaction permit flow or the approval fallback.
redeem.panel_description: The primary path signs an EIP-2612 permit and redeems in a single transaction. If your wallet cannot complete that signature, use the approval fallback below.
redeem.amount_exceeds_balance: The selected amount is higher than your current CFT wallet balance.
redeem.permit_description: Sign a permit message in your wallet and the redemption pool will consume that signature to redeem in a single on-chain transaction.
redeem.fallback_description: If permit signing is unavailable or rejected, approve the redemption pool to spend your CFT and then redeem through the standard allowance flow.
redeem.allowance_warning: Your current CFT allowance is too low for this redemption amount. Approve the exact amount or grant unlimited allowance before redeeming.
redeem.allowance_ready: Allowance is already sufficient for the selected redemption amount.
redeem.how_it_works_line_1: The redemption pool quotes a pro-rata USDC amount for the CFT you select.
redeem.how_it_works_line_2: Permit mode signs an allowance message off-chain and submits redeemWithPermit on-chain in one action.
redeem.how_it_works_line_3: Fallback mode uses ERC-20 approve first, with an unlimited option if you do not want to repeat approvals later.
redeem.wallet_asset_description: If your wallet is connected but CFT is not visible yet, add it here before starting the redemption flow.
